failure_surface: Formula applied to multiple rows (R2:R20) used hardcoded row references (S2, U2) in every cell instead of adjusting per-row, causing all cells to evaluate against the same source row
root_cause: The agent iterated through rows 2-20 and assigned the same literal formula string '=IF(S2="error"...)' to each cell. In Excel/openpyxl, writing identical formula strings to different cells does NOT auto-adjust relative references; each cell retains S2/U2 regardless of its own row position.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When applying formulas to a range of cells where each row needs a different relative reference, dynamically construct the formula for each row using the current row index, or use a single formula assignment with openpyxl's range functionality that preserves relative referencing behavior.
evidence: Test case results show score of 0, indicating complete evaluation failure
Agent code explicitly sets `cell.value = formula` inside a loop where `formula` never changes its row numbers (always S2, U2)
Column S values vary by row (#N/A in row 2, 3, 4...) so S2-only evaluation produces incorrect results for other rows
Openpyxl requires explicit construction of row-specific formulas or use of shared_formula patterns for relative reference behavior

memory_items.title: Relative Reference Construction for Multi-Row Formulas
memory_items.lesson: When writing formulas to multiple rows where each cell needs row-adjusted references, construct the formula string dynamically per row using the row index variable.
memory_items.generalization: Never assign the same formula string to multiple cells expecting Excel-style auto-adjustment. Instead, interpolate the row number into the formula string before assignment.

memory_items.title: Formula Range Application Patterns
memory_items.lesson: For cell ranges requiring formulas with relative references, either write to the first cell and copy, or build each cell's formula independently with its target row number embedded.
memory_items.generalization: openpyxl's cell iteration does not provide automatic relative reference shifting; programmatic row-indexing must be implemented explicitly.

memory_items.title: Validation Before Formula Deployment
memory_items.lesson: After applying formulas to multiple cells, verify that formula strings contain the correct row indices for at least two different cells to confirm dynamic adjustment occurred.
memory_items.generalization: Spot-check formula values across the applied range to catch static-reference errors before considering the operation complete.

edits.content: - **Multi-Row Formula Application**: When applying formulas across a range of rows, construct each formula string dynamically using the current row index. Writing the same formula string to multiple cells will NOT auto-adjust row references in openpyxl. Use f-strings or str.format() to embed row numbers: `f'=IF(S{row}=\"error\", ...)'`.
